title: RFE: anchors in docs.podman.io's man pages would be nice to have
body: I'm not sure it is possible, or easily doable, but it would be nice to have anchors on the man pages that are shown in docs.podman.io.  For instance, I was reviewing @ashley-cui 's blog on timezones and I wanted to link to the tz option there, with something like: `http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-run.1.html#--tz` but I could not.  I had to use a link to the top of the page, and it's a long scroll down.

comments.body: It looks like you can link headers automatically but not content inside the headers, which is seems to be a markdown standard? Otherwise, you can definitely do them, I know regular HTML anchors work on markdown, but you would need to create an anchor for each flag which may be super annoying. Might be a better way, but this is as much as I know now :)
I believe it's been smoldering in the background, but still a nice to have.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@TomSweeneyRedHat @ashley-cui Is this something you think we will ever have?  Or should I close the issue?
@rhatdan I'd love to have it, just a matter of time.  I'd leave it in hopes someone will grab it and run with it.

I have just read up on this.  So you want to take each option and make them into a link
Something like?
```
[**--tz**=*timezone*](#--tz)
```
Sadly gomd2man makes the man page look like
```
man podman-create
...
       --tz=timezone ⟨#--tz⟩
```

Honestly, I've not dived into the syntax that's needed to make it happen, but the end result would be something like what we have on podman.io that @Luap99 (I think) did for us.  I'm not sure if he's a thought or three on this.  So the end result is you'd see a little link symbol next to the titles in the online man page, and whecn clicked on you'd get something like:  https://podman.io/#podman-has-gone-210
